message:
🌱 Add podman-container-tools: Mac: current HEAD errors out on `--add-host` using mission (#1093)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "podman-container-tools-21681-mac-current-head-errors-out-on-add-host-using-host-",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "podman-container-tools: Mac: current HEAD errors out on `--add-host` using `host-gateway` with "host containers internal IP address is empty"",
+    "description": "Mac: current HEAD errors out on `--add-host` using `host-gateway` with "host containers internal IP address is empty". This issue affects 21+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify podman-container-tools troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your podman-container-tools installation:
```bash
podman --version
podman info --format json | jq .version
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review podman-container-tools configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant podman-container-tools configuration:
### Issue Description

With current HEAD (Git hash bbd4476), an error occurs when using the `--add-host` option with `host-gateway` special IP designator, even though this is supposed to be supported with"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Mac: current HEAD errors out on `--add-host` using…",
+        "description": "> I found a workaround so I can handle both docker and podman users:
> 
> ```
> host_gateway=$(podman network inspect -f "{{range .Subnets}}{{.Gateway}}{{end}}" podman 2>/dev/null || echo "host-gateway")
> 
> docker run --add-host foobar:"$host_gateway" -it busybox
> ```

This returns the ip of the
```yaml
podman run --add-host foobar:host-gateway -it busybox
Error: preparing container a90605b73f90a2cab9db7142643a111f010ebabc6d8a74eb0bfeda0f525f97cf for attach: failed to create new hosts file: unable to replace "host-gateway" of host entry "foobar:host-gateway": host containers internal IP address is empty
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Mac: current HEAD errors out on `--add-host`… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test podman-container-tools to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> I found a workaround so I can handle both docker and podman users:
> 
> ```
> host_gateway=$(podman network inspect -f "{{range .Subnets}}{{.Gateway}}{{end}}" podman 2>/dev/null || echo "host-gateway")
> 
> docker run --add-host foobar:"$host_gateway" -it busybox
> ```

This returns the ip of the subnet gateway which is not the host ip for podman machine nor would this ip makes sense for",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "podman run --add-host foobar:host-gateway -it busybox
Error: preparing container a90605b73f90a2cab9db7142643a111f010ebabc6d8a74eb0bfeda0f525f97cf for attach: failed to create new hosts file: unable to replace "host-gateway" of host entry "foobar:host-gateway": host containers internal IP address is empty",
+        "(installed using `brew install podman --HEAD`, resulting in Git hash bbd4476)

podman info

  cgroupControllers:

  cpuUtilization:

  cpus: 4

    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 6.7.4-200.fc39.aarch64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: journald
  memFree:",

Client:       Podman Engine

$ podman run --add-host foobar:host-gateway -it busybox
Error: preparing container 0cbcd3af7f916dde233efbe6410fef54f439395aa14797fe165ecec223a4428d for attach: failed to create new hosts file: unable to replace "host-gateway" of host entry "foobar:host-gateway": host containers internal IP address is empty"
+      ]
